title: Sprites, Groups, Update Contracts, Kill Semantics, and Entity Ownership · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs sprites, groups, update contracts, kill semantics, and entity ownership from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reconciled sprite and group lifecycle after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.
Use separate update, draw-layer, collision, projectile, hazard, and UI groups only when ownership and membership are explicit.
Keep group update inputs consistent or route context through an explicit system boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Sprite Contract · changed case · Review Sprite Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 8062 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile sprite creation, group membership, update and draw order, kill semantics, and entity ownership; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.

Competency probe: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence. Artifact under review: reconciled sprite and group lifecycle.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Layered Groups, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make render order deterministic through LayeredUpdates or explicit sorting and test focus and HUD precedence.
